commands.review_summary:
The PR adds daily per-user/skill/root install-event deduplication and lifecycle cleanup while changing skill search and recommended ranking from downloads to all-time installs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a narrow bug reproduction: the central installs-versus-downloads ranking change is intentional product behavior, while source inspection clearly establishes that current main lacks the proposed deduplication lifecycle.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers must approve or split the ranking policy, oversee semantic conflict resolution against current main, and request final contributor runtime proof.

target.title: Rank skills by installs and harden install telemetry
target.body:
## Summary
- switch recommended listing and skill search popularity from downloads to all-time installs while keeping stars as the first tie-breaker
- add install telemetry dedupe by user, skill, root, and UTC day, plus daily dedupe pruning
- harden telemetry clear/reinstall behavior so batched clears remove old rows without deleting fresh reports
- clean install telemetry dedupe rows during skill hard delete and cover stale recommended cursors, install stat events, and docs/specs

## Local A/B evidence
I ran the baseline PR branch and this branch side by side against the same sanitized prod `skillSearchDigest` + `skills` dataset:

| Variant | URL | Ranking tie-breaker | Public skills |
| --- | --- | --- | ---: |
| Baseline | `http://127.0.0.1:3010/skills` | stars, then downloads | 70,151 |
| Candidate | `http://127.0.0.1:3020/skills` | stars, then installs | 70,151 |

The top 50 recommended rows were unchanged because stars are still the primary key. The first difference appears at rank 77/78 inside the `stars=100` group:

| Rank | Baseline | Candidate |
| ---: | --- | --- |
| 77 | `mx-stocks-screener` — 24,212 downloads, 42 installs | `web-search-plus` — 22,857 downloads, 165 installs |
| 78 | `web-search-plus` — 22,857 downloads, 165 installs | `mx-stocks-screener` — 24,212 downloads, 42 installs |

This is the expected effect: downloads slightly favor `mx-stocks-screener`, while installs favor `web-search-plus` by about 4x.

Additional observed movement in the first returned page:

| Movement | Skill | Stars | Downloads | Installs |
| ---: | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| +3 | `thinking-partner` | 45 | 8,578 | 91 |
| -3 | `web-search-exa` | 45 | 31,062 | 64 |
| -3 | `microsoft-excel` | 41 | 24,278 | 163 |

## Verification
- bunx vitest run convex/search.test.ts convex/skills.listPublicPageV4.test.ts convex/skills.publicListCursor.test.ts convex/telemetry.test.ts convex/crons.test.ts convex/lib/publisherAbuseScoring.test.ts convex/publisherAbuse.test.ts
- bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false
- bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false
- bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false
- bunx convex codegen
- VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid bun run ci:types-build
- NODE_NO_WARNINGS=1 bun run ci:packages
- git diff --check
- AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --output /tmp/clawhub-install-telemetry-autoreview-final.txt
- bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable
- curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:3000/
- bunx convex run skills:listPublicPageV4 '{"numItems":3,"sort":"recommended"}'
- timeout 10 bunx convex logs --history 20
- local A/B: baseline `http://127.0.0.1:3010/skills` and candidate `http://127.0.0.1:3020/skills` both rendered `70.2k` skills from the same prod-shaped dataset

## Local toolchain notes
- bun run ci:static reached peer check, audit, format, and lint cleanly, then failed in knip/oxc-parser with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM on Node 20.16.0.
- bun run ci:unit ran 1,497 tests successfully before Vitest worker startup hit the local html-encoding-sniffer/@exodus/bytes ERR_REQUIRE_ESM issue, causing coverage undercount failure.
- Vite warned that Node 20.16.0 is below its recommended 20.19+ during the successful production build.
